$comment: Project-scoped MCP servers for THIS repo. Merged with the user-level servers in
~/.claude.json; a same-named server here wins, which pins this repo to its own
Omnigraph graph rather than the machine-global default.
Uses the docker bridge, not `npx @modernrelay/omnigraph-mcp`: node/npm/npx are NOT
installed on coding.vm, so an npx bridge cannot start there. docker works on every
Per-project graph isolation: `omnigraph` is pinned to the `agent-skills` graph. A bridge serves
exactly ONE graph: it is chosen by OMNIGRAPH_GRAPH_ID (OMNIGRAPH_GRAPH is the *viewer's*
variable, not this one) and no tool takes a graph argument. Never write this repo's data
There is deliberately NO second `omnigraph-globals` bridge (removed 2026-07-17). It
existed only to read the shared `memory` graph, which holds just two global Preferences
-- TDD-by-default and MCP-first navigation -- and those are already Principles 2 and 6 of
skills/coding-principles/SKILL.md, which every agent loads. A whole extra MCP server to
re-serve two lines was duplication, and it listed every omnigraph tool twice in the
OMNIGRAPH_TOKEN is read from the environment and must never be written here (this file
is tracked). Export it once per machine before launching your agent.
